title: Question: Problem with casez
body:
---


Author Name: **Paul Rolfe**
Original Redmine Message: 1773 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

Our design uses casez statements in the following form for synthesis reasons:

 wire [2:0]  foo
 always @(*) begin
  casez ( {1'b1,foo})
     .....
     {1'b1, 3'b???} begin
                   end
     ......
     default: begin
              end
    endcase
 end

Verilator errors out with "Unsupported: 4-state numbers in this context"

If I change the case to:

 always @(*) begin
  casez ( {1'b1,foo})
     ....
     {1'b1, 3'b000},
     {1'b1, 3'b001},
     {1'b1, 3'b010},
     {1'b1, 3'b011},
     {1'b1, 3'b100},
     {1'b1, 3'b101},
     {1'b1, 3'b110},
     {1'b1, 3'b111}: begin
                    end
     ......
     default: begin
              end
    endcase
 end

Verilator does not generate an error. Is this intended ?  Apart from recoding the case statements, is there any other fix ?

Many Thanks

Paul
